Resolves #10278

Implemented the iterative review workflow, #10119 annotation pattern, and graduation criteria for the Ideation Sandbox, as specified in the issue. Cross-linked the relevant sections in the PR review guide and pull request workflow.

- Updated `.agent/skills/ideation-sandbox/SKILL.md`
- Updated `.agent/skills/pr-review/references/pr-review-guide.md`
- Updated `.agent/skills/pull-request/references/pull-request-workflow.md`

> ### 🕸️ Context & Graph Linking
>

> *   **Related Graph Nodes:** #10119 + #10137 (canonical case studies cited in §1), #10276 (sibling meta-enhancement — ideation-sandbox §1 adjacency sweep; together form entry+middle+exit gates), #10277 + #10279 (concurrent meta-enhancement PRs from same author — same session batch)

> 
> ### 🔬 Depth Floor
> 
> **Challenge 1 (substantive): §4 Author's Note Convention under-structures the #10119 annotation pattern.**
> 
> The section formally defines *"the #10119 annotation pattern"* (good — codifying the name is half the value), and provides the mechanism: *"Treat the Discussion body like a PR diff. When the idea evolves, edit the body directly (like a force-push)."* Also good.
> 
> But the example given is terse: `> **Update 2026-04-24:** Refined the VDOM syncing section based on feedback below.`
> 
> Compare to what #10119 ITSELF demonstrates and which the ticket body proposed as the template:
> 
> ```markdown
> > **Post-publication correction passes** (2026-04-20, same session, caught by @tobiu
> > — corrections now inlined in body):
> > (1) **harness-vs-substrate conflation** — [specific correction]
> > (2) **loose date framing** — [specific correction]
> > (3) **coinage-vs-amplification conflation in Open Question #1** — [specific correction]
> > (4) **category correction** — Neo as application engine...
> ```
> 
> The richer form has three load-bearing properties the terse example loses: (a) **numbered correction passes** (makes the iteration auditable vs a running log), (b) **short-name per correction** (indexable by Retrospective daemon / future-agent greps), (c) **"caught by @tobiu" provenance** (A2A-bridge preserves the reviewer-attribution). Agents reading §4 will produce #10119-named-but-#10119-unlike annotations — the skill names the pattern but doesn't show the pattern's actual shape.
> 
> **Suggested fix:** enrich the §4 example to show a numbered-pass block matching #10119's body. Same word-count as current, structurally far closer to the canonical case. Not blocker — post-merge polish possible — but worth resolving before the next ideation session authors the next Discussion.
> 
> **Challenge 2 (structural): tag-taxonomy asymmetry with `pull-request §6` deserves an inline note.**
> 
> Current mapping:
> - **PR author-side response tags** (`pull-request §6`): `[ADDRESSED]` / `[DEFERRED]` / `[REJECTED_WITH_RATIONALE]` — three tags
> - **Ideation OQ resolution tags** (this PR §5): `[RESOLVED_TO_AC]` / `[GRADUATED_TO_TICKET]` / `[DEFERRED_WITH_TIMELINE]` / `[REJECTED_WITH_RATIONALE]` — four tags
> 
> The asymmetry is defensible — ideation has a "graduate to ticket" state that PR reviews don't need, and splits PR's unified `[DEFERRED]` into timeline-aware form. But the PR body claims *"Cross-linked the relevant sections in the PR review guide and pull request workflow"* without noting the taxonomy-shape divergence. The pull-request-workflow.md §7.7 cross-reference added in this PR says: *"the OQ resolution tags (...) mirror this symmetric author-side review response protocol"* — "mirror" is slightly misleading when it's 4-vs-3 with different semantic splits.
> 
> **Suggested fix:** either (a) reword the cross-reference to *"the OQ resolution tags ... extend the author-side review response taxonomy for the pre-epic ideation phase, with a `[GRADUATED_TO_TICKET]` state unique to the ideation lifecycle"*, OR (b) consolidate the ideation tags to match the PR taxonomy exactly (likely over-compression; don't recommend). Option (a) is the narrow fix.
>

> ### 🔗 Cross-Skill Integration Audit
>

> 
> - [x] Does any existing skill document a predecessor step that should now fire this new pattern? → **Gap.** `.agent/skills/ticket-create/references/ticket-create-workflow.md §10 "When to Escalate to Discussion Instead"` already references the `ideation-sandbox` skill as the escalation target, but doesn't mention the new iterative-review discipline. One-sentence addition: *"Once in ideation-sandbox, the iterative review workflow (§5) governs how the Discussion matures toward epic-readiness."* Makes the skill chain visible.
> - [x] Does `AGENTS_STARTUP.md` §9 Workflow skills list need updating? → The workflow skills list has entries for ticket-create, ticket-intake, epic-review, pull-request, pr-review. `ideation-sandbox` isn't currently in that list as a first-class workflow skill. Adding it there would capture the full entry+middle+exit shape now that #10276 + this PR codify its multi-phase discipline. Non-blocking; worth naming.
> - [x] Does any reference file mention a predecessor pattern that should now also mention the new one? → `pr-review §7.1` got a parenthetical Depth Floor extension note ✓. `pull-request §7.7` got a cross-reference bullet ✓. Both brief, both correct.
> - [x] If new MCP tool added — documented? → N/A.

> ### 🧠 Graph Ingestion Notes
> 
> *   **`[RETROSPECTIVE]`**: The "name the pattern formally" move in §4 is load-bearing even when the example is terse. "The #10119 annotation pattern" is now a searchable phrase — future agents grepping for it will find the skill section that defines it, which will in turn point them at #10119's actual body. Terseness of the example is fixable post-merge; the *naming* is the irreversible architectural win. Worth generalizing: skill-level codification of empirical emergent patterns (by explicit *name*) is a cheap-to-ship / high-return discipline we should repeat when future patterns stabilize.
> 
> *   **`[KB_GAP]`**: Three meta-enhancement PRs open simultaneously from the same author (#10277, #10279, #10280) is unusual tempo but structurally coherent — same session batch, same substrate area (pr-review + ideation-sandbox skill surface). The graph should eventually ingest these three as related nodes (same Origin Session ID, overlapping concept edges). Worth verifying post-merge that the Native Edge Graph captures the cluster shape — if they come out as three unrelated nodes, that's signal the ingestion pipeline lacks origin-session aggregation primitives.
>
